label: Korean
note: Korean is the first pack, not the schema. The engine, the gates marked universal, the error taxonomy and the scorer are language-neutral; everything in this file is what a second language would replace.

phenomena.label: Numeral eaten by a lost pause
phenomena.note: 네 + pause + 다섯 collapses into 네다섯 ("four or five"). Only prosody separates them.

phenomena.label: Address form read as kinship
phenomena.note: 오빠 / 언니 / 형 / 누나 are address forms. Literalising them invents a family.

phenomena.label: Common noun promoted to a name
phenomena.note: 사랑 is both "love" and a given name. A closed cast list is the only reliable disambiguator.

phenomena.label: Contrastive sentence split mid-clause
phenomena.note: A가 아니라 B cut in half and translated separately inverts the claim.

gates.label: silence
gates.rule: No speech in the window means no caption. Undefined entity confidence is junk, not 'not below threshold'. Fail closed.

gates.label: music
gates.rule: Withhold the target line when speech SNR is negative and lexical confidence is under 0.60.

gates.label: repetition
gates.rule: A line that repeats itself more than twice is a decoder loop, not speech.

gates.label: cast
gates.rule: The cast list is closed. A proper noun outside it is demoted to its common-noun reading.

gates.label: address
gates.rule: Address forms are not kinship unless the glossary confirms the family relation.
